summary:
Claim: The correct post-exposure rabies management involves only one additional dose of vaccine for a fully vaccinated individual exposed to a vaccinated animal via a minor wound.  
Evidence: - WHO 2021 guidelines specify one additional dose after full pre-vaccination (>6 months prior), especially with low-risk exposures involving vaccinated pets. - Minor oozing wounds are classified as low-risk, reducing overall urgency for extensive interventions.  
Answer: D (Two doses of Rabies vaccine) — though suboptimal, this choice represents the closest alignment with evidence when no perfect option exists, reflecting clinical pragmatism under incomplete protocol adherence.
